area_anchor: Reception blind-mode variance — no post-deployment measurement path
current_truth: Blind close was introduced to reduce anchoring bias during till/keycard counting, but the loop has no wired post-deployment KPI path to verify discrepancy variance improvement from existing Firebase discrepancy records.
next_scope_now: Run fact-find to define variance KPI contract, baseline vs post-launch windows, extraction/reporting mechanism from Firebase discrepancy records, and weekly review thresholds.
adjacent_later: Segment variance trends by role and shift to isolate where blind mode has strongest effect.
Add automated regression alert when post-launch variance deteriorates versus baseline.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: self-improvement path needs measurable blind-mode variance tracking
why: The feature intent is bias reduction, but without a standing measurement loop the team cannot confirm impact or decide whether to iterate the workflow.

intended_outcome.statement: A reproducible weekly blind-mode variance report exists from Firebase discrepancy records, with baseline-vs-post-launch percent change and explicit threshold-based follow-up rules.
